description: Profile page: avatar upload, name editing, telegram/email linking, and display of connected accounts (Google/Telegram)
steps: Create a new API route at src/app/api/profile/route.ts with: GET - returns full user profile (name, email, image, telegramId, telegramUsername, googleId, createdAt, role). PATCH - updates user profile fields (name, image). Use auth() for authentication and db from @/lib/db.

Create a new API route at src/app/api/profile/link-telegram/route.ts with POST - links a Telegram account to the current Google user. Accepts telegramId and verifies a token similar to the auth flow (or uses a simpler linking flow where user enters their Telegram username and confirms via bot).

Rewrite src/app/[locale]/(main)/profile/page.tsx with full functionality: (1) Fetch user profile from /api/profile on mount. (2) Avatar section: show current avatar with a change button that uses UploadThing (the 'avatar' endpoint is already configured in src/app/api/uploadthing/core.ts). After upload, call PATCH /api/profile with new image URL. (3) Name editing: click edit icon shows an input field, save calls PATCH /api/profile. (4) Connected accounts section: show Google as connected if googleId exists (with email), show Telegram as connected if telegramId exists (with username). If not connected, show a 'Link' button. (5) For Google linking - redirect to sign in with Google (merge accounts). (6) For Telegram linking - show bot link (@EduPrimeuzbot) with instructions to use /link command.

Add avatar upload using the @uploadthing/react package which is already in dependencies. Use the UploadButton or custom upload handler to upload to the 'avatar' endpoint. After successful upload, update the user's image in the database via PATCH /api/profile.
Show createdAt date formatted nicely in the profile. Show user role (TEACHER/USER/ADMIN) with appropriate badge.
acceptance_criteria: Profile page loads and shows user's current data from database
User can upload a new avatar image and it updates immediately
User can edit their display name and save it
Connected accounts section shows which providers are linked (Google with email, Telegram with username)
Unlinked providers show a helpful message on how to link them
Profile shows the registration date and user role
npm run build succeeds without errors
verification: Run npm run build and confirm no TypeScript errors
findings: Profile page fully rewritten with avatar upload (UploadThing), name editing, connected accounts display (Google/Telegram). API route created at /api/profile with GET and PATCH endpoints. Used existing UploadButton from @/lib/uploadthing which was already configured. Build passes cleanly.
